fkcompute
  • API Reference
  • fkcompute.inversion — Sign Assignment Search
  • Edit on GitHub

fkcompute.inversion — Sign Assignment Search

The inversion subpackage implements Phase 1 of the computation pipeline: finding a valid \(\pm 1\) sign assignment for the strands of a braid.

Entry Point

fkcompute.inversion.api.find_sign_assignment(braid: List[int], degree: int, partial_signs: Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None = None, max_workers: int = 1, chunk_size: int = 16384, include_flip: bool = True, max_shifts: int | None = None, verbose: bool = False) → InversionResult[source]

Find a valid sign assignment for the given braid and degree.

For homogeneous braids, the sign assignment is determined automatically. For fibered braids, searches through braid variants and sign combinations.

Parameters:

  • braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

  • degree – Degree bound for the computation.

  • partial_signs – Optional partial sign constraints.

  • max_workers – Number of parallel workers for the search.

  • chunk_size – Number of permutation candidates per worker task (internally capped for IPC safety).

  • include_flip – Whether to include flipped braid variants.

  • max_shifts – Maximum number of cyclic shifts to try.

  • verbose – If True, print progress during search.

Returns:

Result with success status, braid, sign assignment, and metadata.

Return type:

InversionResult

Result Type

class fkcompute.inversion.api.InversionResult(success: bool, braid: List[int], sign_assignment: Dict[int, List[int]] | None, braid_type: str, degree: int)[source]

Bases: object

Result of the sign assignment search.

success

Whether a valid sign assignment was found.

Type:

bool

braid

The (possibly transformed) braid word.

Type:

List[int]

sign_assignment

The sign assignment dict, or None if not found.

Type:

Dict[int, List[int]] | None

braid_type

“homogeneous” or “fibered”.

Type:

str

degree

The degree used for the search.

Type:

int

Example:

from fkcompute.inversion.api import find_sign_assignment

result = find_sign_assignment([1, -2, 1, -2], degree=3, max_workers=2)

if result.success:
    print("Braid type:", result.braid_type)         # "fibered"
    print("Sign assignment:", result.sign_assignment)
    print("Braid used:", result.braid)

    # Convert to the legacy dict format used by _fk_compute
    d = result.to_result_dict()
else:
    print("Search failed — try a higher degree or more workers")
success: bool
braid: List[int]
sign_assignment: Dict[int, List[int]] | None
braid_type: str
degree: int
to_result_dict() → dict[source]

Convert to the legacy result dictionary format.

Braid Type Detection

Parallel sign assignment search for FK computation.

This module provides functions for searching through sign assignment configurations in parallel to find valid assignments.

fkcompute.inversion.search.worker_on_perm_batch(perms: List[List[int]]) → Tuple[List[int], Dict[int, List[int]]] | None[source]
class fkcompute.inversion.search.BraidType(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)[source]

Bases: Enum

Classification of braids used by this module.

HOMOGENEOUS = 0
FIBERED = 1
fkcompute.inversion.search.braid_type(braid: List[int]) → int[source]

Classify a braid as homogeneous or fibered.

Parameters:

braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

Returns:

BraidType.HOMOGENEOUS.value if homogeneous, else BraidType.FIBERED.value.

Return type:

int

fkcompute.inversion.search.total_assignments_for_braid(braid_states: BraidStates, partial_signs: Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None = None) → int[source]

Return the total number of sign assignments for this braid.

fkcompute.inversion.search.split_ranges(n: int, chunk_size: int) → Iterator[Tuple[int, int]][source]

Yield half-open integer ranges [start, end) covering 0..n-1.

fkcompute.inversion.search.sign_assignment_from_index(index: int, braid_states: BraidStates, partial_signs: Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None = None) → Dict[int, List[int]][source]

Decode an integer into per-component +/-1 sign lists.

The binary expansion of index is interpreted bitwise via sign = 2*bit - 1.

fkcompute.inversion.search.check_assignment_for_braid(index: int, degree: int, braid_state: BraidStates, partial_signs: Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None = None) → Tuple[List[int], Dict[int, List[int]]] | None[source]

Test a single sign assignment (by index) for a given braid.

Returns:

(braid, strand_signs) on success, otherwise None.

Return type:

tuple[list[int], dict] or None

fkcompute.inversion.search.worker_on_range(args: Tuple[int, BraidStates, int, int, bool, Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None]) → Tuple[List[int], Dict[int, List[int]]] | None[source]

Worker for a range of indices; returns the first success in [start, end).

fkcompute.inversion.search.parallel_try_sign_assignments(degree: int, braid_state: BraidStates, partial_signs: Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None = None, max_workers: int | None = None, chunk_size: int = 16384, include_flip: bool = True, max_shifts: int | None = None, verbose: bool = False) → Tuple[List[int], Dict[int, List[int]]] | Literal[False][source]

Parallel search for a consistent sign assignment across braid variants.

This search enumerates only sign assignments induced by permutation candidates from fkcompute.inversion.permutations.iter_perms_rot_closed(). The chunk_size parameter controls how many candidate permutations are bundled into each worker task.

Returns:

(braid_variant, strand_signs) on success, else False.

Return type:

(list[int], dict) or bool

Parallel Search

The full parallel search is exposed through fkcompute.inversion.search.parallel_try_sign_assignments(). It returns False if no valid assignment was found, or a (braid, sign_assignment) tuple on success.

from fkcompute.domain.braid.states import BraidStates
from fkcompute.inversion.search import parallel_try_sign_assignments

bs = BraidStates([1, -2, 1, -2])
sol = parallel_try_sign_assignments(
    degree=3,
    braid_states=bs,
    partial_signs=None,
    max_workers=4,
    chunk_size=1 << 12,
    include_flip=False,
)
if sol:
    braid, signs = sol
    print(signs)
fkcompute.inversion.search.parallel_try_sign_assignments(degree: int, braid_state: BraidStates, partial_signs: Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None = None, max_workers: int | None = None, chunk_size: int = 16384, include_flip: bool = True, max_shifts: int | None = None, verbose: bool = False) → Tuple[List[int], Dict[int, List[int]]] | Literal[False][source]

Parallel search for a consistent sign assignment across braid variants.

This search enumerates only sign assignments induced by permutation candidates from fkcompute.inversion.permutations.iter_perms_rot_closed(). The chunk_size parameter controls how many candidate permutations are bundled into each worker task.

Returns:

(braid_variant, strand_signs) on success, else False.

Return type:

(list[int], dict) or bool

Braid Variants

Braid variant generation for sign assignment search.

This module provides functions for generating braid variants (rotations and flips) used during the parallel sign assignment search.

fkcompute.inversion.variants.flip(braid: List[int]) → List[int][source]

Mirror (flip) the braid’s generator indices.

Parameters:

braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

Returns:

The flipped braid word.

Return type:

list[int]

fkcompute.inversion.variants.rotate(braid: List[int]) → List[int][source]

Rotate the braid word left by one generator.

Parameters:

braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

Returns:

The rotated braid word with the first generator moved to the end.

Return type:

list[int]

fkcompute.inversion.variants.flip_coordinate(coord: Tuple[int, int], n_strands: int) → Tuple[int, int][source]

Mirror a lattice coordinate (i, j) horizontally for an n-strand braid.

fkcompute.inversion.variants.component_locations(braid: List[int]) → List[List[Tuple[int, int]]][source]

Traverse the braid closure and return a list of components, each as an ordered list of lattice coordinates (i,j).

fkcompute.inversion.variants.build_coord_signs(braid: List[int], strand_signs: Dict[int, List[int]]) → Dict[Tuple[int, int], int][source]

Expand strand_signs into a coordinate->sign dictionary.

fkcompute.inversion.variants.collapse_coord_signs(coord_signs: Dict[Tuple[int, int], int], braid: List[int]) → Dict[int, List[int]][source]

Collapse coordinate->signs into strand_signs (component-indexed cyclic lists).

fkcompute.inversion.variants.flip_partial_signs(braid: List[int], strand_signs: Dict[int, List[int]]) → Dict[int, List[int]][source]

Perform a horizontal flip of an ansatz sign assignment.

fkcompute.inversion.variants.rotate_partial_signs(braid: List[int], strand_signs: Dict[int, List[int]]) → Dict[int, List[int]][source]

Perform a rotation of an ansatz sign assignment.

class fkcompute.inversion.variants.VariantMeta[source]

Bases: TypedDict

flipped: bool
shift: int
fkcompute.inversion.variants.generate_braid_variants(braid_state: BraidStates, partial_signs: Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None = None, include_flip: bool = True, max_shifts: int | None = None) → Iterator[Tuple[BraidStates, Dict[int, List[int | None]] | None, VariantMeta]][source]

Enumerate braid variants by cyclic rotation (and optional flipping).

Yields:

(BraidStates, partial_signs, meta) – Tuples of (variant_braid_state, partial_signs, meta) where meta = {“flipped”: bool, “shift”: int}.

Permutation Candidates

Permutation generation and sign-diagram conversion for braid arc labels.

Ports the following Mathematica functions from ImproveSignDiagramSearch-Davide.nb:

topstrandQ, firstAppearance, lastAppearanceQ, nextgeneratorpos, labelCrossings, NewRot, extendAndFilter, permsRot, permsRotClosed, permToSigns.

Arc labels are 1-indexed throughout (matching Mathematica semantics). Crossing positions j are also 1-indexed.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.label_crossings(braid: List[int]) → List[List[int]][source]

Label arc segments at every corner of every crossing.

Returns an n×4 matrix (list of lists) where entry [i][c] is the arc-segment label at corner c+1 of crossing i+1.

Labels are 1-indexed. For a knot (single component) they run from 1 to 2n+1 where n = len(braid). For links with multiple components the range extends to 2n+c, where c is the number of component cycles induced by the entry-corner successor map.

Direct port of Mathematica labelCrossings.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.new_rot(braid: List[int]) → List[List[int]][source]

Return a list of [sign, overstrand_label, understrand_label] per crossing.

For a positive crossing the overstrand labels are corners 1,2; for a negative crossing they are corners 2,1 (swapped).

Direct port of Mathematica NewRot.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.perms_rot(braid: List[int]) → List[List[int]][source]

Generate all valid arc-label permutations for the open braid.

Each permutation is a 1-indexed list of length 2n+1 (n = len(braid)) where entry i is the image of arc label i under the permutation.

The final arc 2n+1 maps only to itself (open-strand convention).

Port of Mathematica permsRot.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.perms_rot_closed(braid: List[int]) → List[List[int]][source]

Generate all valid arc-label permutations for the closed braid.

Same as perms_rot() except the final arc 2n+1 may also map to 1 (closure allows the open strand to loop back).

Port of Mathematica permsRotClosed.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.iter_perms_rot(braid: List[int]) → Iterator[List[int]][source]

Iterate valid arc-label permutations for the open braid.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.iter_perms_rot_closed(braid: List[int]) → Iterator[List[int]][source]

Iterate valid arc-label permutations for the closed braid.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.perm_to_signs_metadata(braid: List[int]) → tuple[List[int], Dict[int, int], int][source]

Precompute metadata needed for fast perm -> signs conversion.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.compile_perm_to_signs(braid: List[int]) → Callable[[List[int]], Dict[int, List[int]]][source]

Return a fast converter perm -> strand_signs for this braid.

fkcompute.inversion.permutations.perm_to_signs(perm: List[int], braid: List[int]) → Dict[int, List[int]][source]

Convert a 1-indexed permutation to a dict mapping component index → list of ±1 signs.

Arc labels used by new_rot() are assigned to link components using BraidTopology. Signs within each component are collected in arc-label order (ascending).

Port of Mathematica permToSigns, extended to multi-component links.

Sign Validation

Sign assignment feasibility checking for FK computation.

This module provides functions for validating sign assignments against constraint relations during the Phase 1 inversion search.

fkcompute.inversion.validation.check_sign_assignment(degree: int, relations: List, braid_states) → Dict | None[source]

Check if a sign assignment is valid for a given degree.

Parameters:

  • degree – Degree bound to check.

  • relations – List of reduced constraint relations.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object with sign assignment.

Returns:

Dictionary with criteria, multi_var_inequalities, single_var_signs, and assignment if valid, None otherwise.

Return type:

dict or None
